overview: The Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS) aims to provide financial assistance to startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, market entry, and commercialization. The scheme is sector-agnostic and will support startups across all sectors.
eligibility: A startup, recognized by DPIIT, incorporated not more than 2 years ago at the time of application, with a business idea to develop a product or service with market fit, viable commercialization, and scope of scaling
benefits: Financial assistance up to Rs. 20 Lakhs as grant for validation of Proof of Concept, or prototype development, or product trials, and up to Rs. 50 Lakhs of investment for market entry, commercialization, or scaling up through convertible debentures or debt or debt-linked instruments
financial_support: The Seed Fund will be disbursed to eligible startups through eligible incubators across India
application_process: Online application on the Startup India portal, with evaluation by the Incubator Seed Management Committee (ISMC) and selection by the Experts Advisory Committee (EAC)
deadlines: Rolling basis, with no fixed deadline
implementing_agency: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), Ministry of Commerce and Industry

overview: The Startup India Initiative is a flagship program of the Government of India, launched in 2016 by DPIIT to foster innovation, support entrepreneurs, and drive sustainable economic growth. The initiative provides various benefits, including tax exemptions, easier compliance, and funding support, to eligible startups.
eligibility: Private Limited Company, LLP, or Registered Partnership with turnover < INR 100 Cr, incorporated < 10 years ago, working on innovative products/services/processes.
benefits: DPIIT recognition, 3-year income tax exemption under 80-IAC, self-certification under 9 labour laws, faster patent examination at 80% rebated fees.
financial_support: Fund of Funds for Startups (FFS) of INR 10,000 Cr managed by SIDBI invests in SEBI-registered Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs) that fund startups.
grant_amount_per_entity: Up to INR 20 Lakhs from SISFS for early-stage validation
application_process: 1. Register on startupindia.gov.in 2. Fill DPIIT recognition form with business description 3. Upload Certificate of Incorporation, PAN, pitch deck 4. Submit and await approval (typically 2-7 business days).
deadlines: Rolling basis — no fixed deadline. Applications are accepted year-round.
implementing_agency: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), Ministry of Commerce and Industry

overview: The Credit Guarantee Scheme for Startups (CGSS) provides credit guarantees to loans extended by Member Institutions to finance eligible startups, with a maximum guarantee cover of ₹20 crore per borrower.
eligibility: The startup should be recognized by DPIIT as per Gazette Notifications issued from time to time, and should not be in default to any lending/investing institution and not classified as Non-Performing Asset as per RBI guidelines.
benefits: The scheme provides a credit guarantee of up to ₹20 crore per borrower, with a guarantee cover of 85% of the amount in default for loan amounts up to ₹10 crore, and 75% of the amount in default for loan amounts exceeding ₹10 crore.
financial_support: The scheme provides a credit guarantee to loans extended by Member Institutions, with a maximum guarantee cover of ₹20 crore per borrower.
application_process: Startups can apply for the scheme through the Jan Samarth Portal or by approaching the nearest branch of any Member Lending Institution directly.
implementing_agency: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), Ministry of Commerce and Industry

overview: The Fund of Funds for Startups (FFS) is a government-backed investment fund that provides funding to startups in India. The scheme is managed by the Small Industries Development Bank of India (SIDBI) and has a corpus of INR 10,000 crores. The fund invests in SEBI-registered Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs) that in turn invest in startups.
eligibility: Startups that are registered in India and meet the eligibility criteria set by the government can apply for funding under the FFS scheme.
benefits: The FFS scheme provides funding to startups, which can be used to develop their business, hire employees, and expand their operations. The scheme also provides mentorship and networking opportunities to startups.
financial_support: The FFS scheme provides funding to startups through SEBI-registered AIFs. The fund invests in AIFs that in turn invest in startups.
application_process: Startups can apply for funding under the FFS scheme through the Startup India website. The application process involves submitting a business plan, financial projections, and other relevant documents.
deadlines: The application process for the FFS scheme is open throughout the year. However, the government may announce specific deadlines for applying to the scheme.
implementing_agency: Small Industries Development Bank of India (SIDBI)
